IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is running hard on Polaris Forge Data Center with Parsons Electric stacking **$44.80/hr plus $100–$150/day non-taxed per diem** on 6-10s. That's real money in Harwood if you've got 100+ miles GPS radius clearance and can move for 50–78 hand calls across Hunt Electric and Apex Line Contractors too. Book depth is thin (130 hands on 26 calls)—you'll work.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** has Port Washington Data Center locked down across Gurtz, MJ Electric, and Uihlein—**JW wage plus $10/hr premium** on valid WI license, 50–58 hours weekly. Book sits at 172 hands against 36 calls; steady inside JW work, no data center crunch like some locals.

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** posts **$54.75 scale** with Meade Electric's Meta Data Center pushing **10% above scale** on July 15 & 20, plus 5-12s Mon-Friday. Google Data Center Mooresville (Ermco, new construction) and IU Hospital (Industrial Electric, indoor AC, OSHA 1) round out 41 calls. Book depth 204 hands—moderate pressure.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** Microsoft Mount Pleasant is nearly locked: Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, Kelso Burnett all calling **$62.51/hr** on long sleeves and safety toe, WI reciprocal or 2+ verifiable years. Only 11 calls, 76 hands—if you're in the door, you're golden.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** Lake Mariner Data (O'Connell, Ferguson) and Amazon (BCU) both **$48.20/hr** with PD available, 7/10s first shift or 5/10s + Saturday. Book 103 hands, 9 calls—tight ratio favoring speed-of-call labor.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** holds 38 calls on QTS Fayette, Wellstar Paulding, and Plant Yates (Beltline Electric, **$42.18/hr**, 5-10s 6:30am SCMMA) but tops at **$48.00 scale**. Cable splicer calls require 3 years documented experience.

Data center work is pulling the migration hard—Wisconsin, Indiana, North Dakota, and the Northeast corridor are where the hands are flowing.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** is running hot with 48 calls and roughly 133 hands on the books. NEW RIVER ELECT. CORP. is the anchor, pushing 41 journeymen into Columbus distribution work at **$61.44/hr** with **$125/day per diem** and a **$100 bonus if you work every available shift.** Same outfit has 10 more south toward Chillicothe. The kicker: THAYER POWER & COMM. is stacking make-ready and VVO reconduct calls with **$500 for 45-hour weeks and $600 for 55-hour weeks**—hotels paid. Book depth is real here; don't sleep on it.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has 26 calls chasing 130 hands, but watch the pay floor. Parsons Electric dominates the Polaris Forge data center (78 slots) at **$44.80/hr** with 6-10s and **$100/day per diem**, but that's helper-rate work. Apex Line Contractors is the journeyman play—**$58.50/hr, 6-12s, $100/day incentive**—on a smaller crew. North Dakota reciprocates lineman licenses in 30 days.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** sits quiet at 10 calls but pays clean: Summit and Cupertino are running inside journeymen at **$55/hr** (NM State JIW License EE98J required) with **all OT double time.** Rosendin undercuts at $40 base but dangles a **$15/hr incentive for hitting 90% of a 60-hour week.**

**LU-291 Boise, ID** has META and Micron PLA work bleeding into the summer. VECA Electric is the premium call—**$65.33/hr, META MOUs locked in**—while Lea Electric's Micron play sits at **$45.33/hr but stacks $170/day per diem** (7 days if eligible) plus **$20/hr travel pay.**

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** is bone-dry: 46 calls chasing only 60 hands. Golden Valley has an 8-week organizing gig at $30.86/hr, and Morley's Delta Junction inside work includes housing. Pay is scattered ($24–$91k annually depending on the call), but the book-to-call ratio is your friend here.

**Data centers and Polaris Forge are vacuuming hands across the dispatch—transmission and distribution rebuild work is thinning out fast.**
